method_card.parent_question: Can an LLM judge reproduce operator-lineage review verdicts on existing live-pilot artifacts, and on which review axes?
intended_inputs: eleven existing pilots' local output traces and their three previously approved source excerpts
fifteen committed human evaluation records as ground truth (never shown to the judge)
pre-registered blinding procedure, fixed 17-trial set, agreement metrics, and delegation thresholds
mechanically extracted span text per locator candidate as judge input
DeepSeek V4 Pro with thinking enabled and a reasoning-aware 8000-token cap
pre-recorded prompt and model configuration metadata
intended_outputs: per-axis agreement table (verdict, score, tags, locator labels)
per-axis delegation labels: delegable, assistive, or not_delegable
known_risks: This is a proposed live LLM pilot planning record, not a completed run.
Verdict leakage through overlooked prompt content would invalidate trials; the harness asserts blinding before each call.
The judge may pass negative controls (sycophancy), disqualifying axes from delegation.
Fifteen ground-truth records is a small sample; results are provisional by construction.
Judge and reviewed artifacts share a model family (DeepSeek V4), risking shared blind spots; recorded as a study limitation.
Prompt traces contain source and artifact text and must stay in ignored local paths.
non_goals: not review authority over any existing EvaluationRecord
not a new protocol evaluator type
